channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Comelec categorizes 27 BARMM areas as high-risk ahead of parliamentary elections
item.description: The Commission on Elections (Comelec) has classified 27 areas in the Bangsamoro Autonomous Region in Muslim Mindanao (BARMM) under the 'red category' due to serious threats and a history of election-r...

item.title: Minimum Liability Coverage Requirements for Car Insurance in Ontario and Other Provinces
item.description: The article explains the mandatory liability insurance requirements for car owners in Canada, focusing on Ontario's minimum $200,000 coverage but emphasizing that experts recommend higher limits like ...

item.title: FIFA World Cup Halftime Show Aims to Unite Global Fans Through Music and Fundraising
item.description: The 2026 FIFA World Cup will feature its first-ever halftime show, a 11-minute spectacle designed to blend global music talent with FIFA's fundraising goals. Collaborating with artists like Beyoncé, N...

item.title: NASA's New Horizons Probe Resumes Science Operations in the Kuiper Belt After Hibernation
item.description: NASA's New Horizons spacecraft, launched in 2006 and famously flown by Pluto in 2015, has resumed scientific operations after emerging from a planned hibernation mode. The probe, now 5.9 billion miles...

item.title: England's Third-Place Finish at 2026 World Cup Sparks Reflection on Pride and Performance
item.description: England manager Thomas Tuchel expressed hope that his players would one day look back on their third-place finish at the 2026 FIFA World Cup with pride, despite falling short of their goal to win the ...

item.title: Peter Obi Visits Abuja Supermarket to Shop and Engage with Locals
item.description: Former presidential candidate Peter Obi was spotted shopping at a H-Medix Supermarket in Abuja, where he interacted with staff and shoppers. Esther Umoh, a former campaign photographer for the NDC, sh...

item.title: Truck Driver Killed During Ol Kalou By-Election as Leaders Condemn Violence
item.description: Two men were killed during the Ol Kalou Constituency by-election in Nyandarua County after armed men stormed polling stations, sparking widespread condemnation from local leaders. The victims included...

item.title: Research Reveals Coffee's Health Benefits Linked to Cellular Receptor Activation
item.description: Scientists from Texas A&M University have uncovered how certain compounds in coffee may protect cells from stress, inflammation, and damage by activating the NR4A1 receptor. This receptor plays a crit...

item.title: Pauline Hanson's Remarks on Migration and Cultural Assimilation Spark Controversy
item.description: Pauline Hanson, leader of the One Nation Party, made controversial remarks at the UK Conservative Political Action Conference (CPAC), urging Australians not to apologise for being white. She criticize...

item.title: India's Economic Policies and Political Dynamics
item.description: The article discusses the Indian government's focus on economic reforms and political strategies to drive growth. It highlights the balance between fiscal policies, infrastructure development, and add...

item.title: Understanding Super El Nino and Its Effects on Canadian Weather Patterns
item.description: The article discusses the potential impacts of a 'super El Niño' event on Canadian weather patterns, focusing on extreme climate conditions such as prolonged droughts in southern regions, increased ra...

item.title: Integrated Transport Strategy for Kigoma's Economic Growth
item.description: President Samia Suluhu Hassan is set to launch the Standard Gauge Railway (SGR) from Tabora to Kigoma, a project that has long been a priority for the region. However, the success of this railway depe...

item.title: Footballer Ramos and his wife respond to fan's controversial comment about their relationship
item.description: The article details a situation involving Spanish footballer Sergio Ramos and his wife, Pau Català, following a fan's provocative comment on social media. The fan's message, which suggested that Ramos...

item.title: Suspect Arrested for Aerial Fire Near Minister's Vehicle, Safe City Tech Used
item.description: A young man has been arrested for allegedly firing aerial shots near the vehicle of Information Minister Attaullah Tarar in Lahore's Defence area. Police tracked down the suspect using Safe City surve...

item.title: Wife of hunger-striking activist Sonam Wangchuk alleges illegal detention after hospital transfer
item.description: Indian activist Sonam Wangchuk, who has been on a hunger strike since June 28 to protest alleged irregularities and question paper leaks in a major university entrance examination, was shifted to a go...

item.title: Tanzania-Egypt Forum Discusses Trade and Investment Growth Strategies
item.description: The Tanzania-Egypt forum convened to outline strategies for enhancing trade relations and fostering investment growth between the two nations. Discussions focused on identifying key sectors for collab...

item.title: Tamil Writer D Ravikumar's Stories Explore Contemporary Dalit Struggles
item.description: D Ravikumar's collection of short stories, 'An Ocean in a Well,' delves into the harsh realities faced by Dalits through personal narratives. The tales highlight systemic injustice, caste-based oppres...

item.title: Sam Burns Surpasses Expectations, Takes Lead in British Open After Last-Minute Decision
item.description: Sam Burns, a New Zealand golfer, has taken the lead in the British Open after a dramatic last-minute decision to compete. Two weeks after his daughter's birth and just a week before the tournament, Bu...

item.title: US-Iran Conflict's Impact on Australia's Labour Market and Unemployment Rate
item.description: The article discusses the potential economic implications of the escalating US-Iran conflict on Australia's labour market and unemployment rate. Despite resilient jobs data, the prolonged Middle East ...

item.title: Ferry Capsizes Off Guyana Coast, 8 Survivors Rescued Amid Ongoing Search
item.description: An emergency rescue operation is underway after the passenger ferry MV Barima capsized off the coast of Guyana, leaving 116 people stranded at sea. The vessel departed Port Kaituma on Saturday afterno...
